mechanism_narrative: With FEDFUNDS pinned near zero (0.09%), the discount rate applied to equity cash flows remains structurally compressed, mechanically supporting higher present values for future earnings and producing a persistent positive drift in S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon as long as rates stay near the zero lower bound.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and forward 60-day SPX return falls below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or SPX drifts negative for two consecutive quarters while FEDFUNDS remains below 0.25%.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in UNRATE exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals accelerating labor market healing, which raises expected consumer spending and corporate revenue, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the following month as investors price in improving fundamentals.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls by more than 0.2pp month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 6.3 percent as of 2021-01, down from pandemic peak
failure_condition: In triggered months, forward 20-day SPX returns are negative in more than 45% of instances, or the effect disappears once controlling for concurrent PAYEMS surprises (partial correlation < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS (retail sales) and HOUST (housing starts) both show month-over-month increases simultaneously, it indicates broad-based consumer and investment demand recovery that jointly signals durable economic expansion, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next month; neither series alone reliably predicts this effect because retail strength without housing follow-through often reflects transitory stimulus rather than sustained demand.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 540915 million and HOUST at 1669 thousand units as of latest snapshot
failure_condition: Joint positive RSAFS/HOUST momentum fails to outperform either single-series signal in out-of-sample Rank IC by more than 0.01, or the joint signal's hit rate falls below 55% over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a zero-lower-bound regime, a rise in CPIAUCSL accompanied by persistently near-zero FEDFUNDS raises market expectations of future policy tightening and inflation risk premia, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter as investors discount the eventual withdrawal of monetary accommodation; this mechanism is regime-specific because in a tightening-cycle regime the same CPI rise would already be priced into rates and would not generate the same equity reaction.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL year-over-year change exceeds 2% while FEDFUNDS remains below 0.25%
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 262.231 and FEDFUNDS at 0.09 as of 2021-01
regime_hint: zero lower bound with post-recession inflation reacceleration
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns remain positive in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the negative relationship disappears once FEDFUNDS rises above 0.5% within the sample.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained month-over-month increase in INDPRO reflects expanding industrial capacity utilization and supply-side recovery, which supports near-term corporate earnings expectations and produces a modest positive drift in S&P 500 returns over the following trading week.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 105.7244 as of 2020-12, recovering from pandemic trough
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO monthly change and forward 5-day SPX return stays below 0.02 over any rolling 1-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A payroll surprise where PAYEMS increases by more than 300,000 in a single month signals a stronger-than-expected labor market rebound, triggering a short-term positive equity reaction over the following week as markets price in stronger aggregate demand and reduced recession risk.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 142631 thousand persons as of 2021-01
failure_condition: Following triggered months, forward 5-day SPX returns are negative in more than 50% of instances, or effect size is not statistically distinguishable from non-triggered months (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A quarter-over-quarter acceleration in GDPC1 above the prior trailing four-quarter average signals a durable output recovery that lifts long-horizon earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth exceeds trailing 4-quarter average by more than 1 percentage point
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 18780.325 billion chained dollars as of 2020-Q3/Q4
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns following GDPC1 acceleration are not significantly higher than unconditional baseline returns (t-test p > 0.05) over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month CPIAUCSL increase exceeding 0.3% signals an inflation surprise that raises rate-hike expectations even at low nominal FEDFUNDS levels, causing a short-term negative S&P 500 reaction over the following month as valuation multiples compress in anticipation of tighter future policy.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC between CPI surprise and forward returns is below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window.
